meaning: n.[解] 横隔膜,控光装置,照相机镜头上的光圈,(电话等)振动膜
examples: The diaphragm vibrates, thus setting the air around it in motion.
In addition, breathing during REM depends much more on the action of the diaphragm and much less on rib cage action.
The diaphragm, shutter speed and menu can be changed quickly using the command dial.
This allows it to vibrate in sympathy with sound waves, like the diaphragm in a conventional microphone.
He underwent two surgeries after suffering a single gunshot wound that penetrated his chest, diaphragm, stomach and liver.
